sourceMovie.title: Congo
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Congo works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Carter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Congo clicked for you, John Carter (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Congo clicked for you, Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Congo?
faq.answer: Try John Carter, Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes, Planet of the Apes—each shares DNA with Congo while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Congo?
faq.answer: Congo (1995) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Congo worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
